text: War Monitor: Oil markets saw historic intraday swing on Day 24 — Brent hit /barrel in early trading, crashed below  on Trump's ceasefire claim (largest single-day % drop of the war), settled at .44. IEA chief Birol confirmed this is the largest oil supply disruption in modern history. Goldman Sachs warns: if Hormuz flows stay at 5% of normal for 10 weeks, prices will likely exceed the 2008 record of /barrel.
verificationNote: Brent  peak and .44 close confirmed by Bloomberg. IEA Birol statement confirmed. Goldman Sachs forecast from Bloomberg.

text: Intel Crab: Netanyahu reportedly frustrated Mossad's promises of a popular uprising in Iran have not materialized — per NYT citing current and former US/Israeli intelligence officials. Netanyahu has called on world leaders to join the coalition, warning Iran's Diego Garcia IRBM tests prove it has 4,000km+ range reaching deep into Europe. Israel and US remain aligned operationally, but divergences on war's end-state are widening.
verificationNote: Netanyahu frustration with Mossad uprising prediction confirmed by Times of Israel. Diego Garcia capability warning confirmed by Jerusalem Post and NBC.

text: CONFIRMED: Iranian ballistic missile impacted residential area in central Tel Aviv overnight — 100kg warhead detonated at street level. NPR journalists confirmed crater on scene. Apartment facade mangled, multiple vehicles crushed. 4 lightly injured. One of only a handful of unintercepted strikes to actually hit Tel Aviv proper this conflict.
verificationNote: On-scene NPR reporting confirmed. Times of Israel and Jerusalem Post also confirmed.

text: IDF overnight strike package on Tehran hit 6 distinct targets including the IRGC Security HQ, Intelligence Ministry HQ, and Khatam-al Anbiya military emergency command. IDF now says 330 of Iran's ~470 ballistic missile launchers destroyed or disabled. Rate of Iranian fire has slowed from ~90/day on Day 1 to ~10/day now — but IDF planning at least 3 more weeks of operations.
verificationNote: IDF statement confirmed by Times of Israel and Jerusalem Post.

text: MAJOR DIPLOMATIC DEVELOPMENT: Lebanon has declared Iran's incoming ambassador Mohammad Reza Shibani persona non grata and ordered him to leave by March 29. Lebanon also recalled its own ambassador from Tehran. Lebanese FM cited IRGC's direct command of Hezbollah as violating diplomatic norms. This is a watershed — Lebanon formally blaming Iran for drawing it into the war at cost of 1,000+ killed and 1 million displaced.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Al Jazeera, The National, Times of Israel.

text: EXCLUSIVE: Araghchi privately told US envoys Witkoff and Kushner that he has Khamenei's approval to 'close the issue quickly' if Iran's conditions are met — despite Ghalibaf's simultaneous public denial of any talks. Significant gaps remain. Israel was excluded from these contacts. An Islamabad meeting between Ghalibaf's team and Witkoff/Kushner is being arranged for this week via Pakistani intermediaries.
verificationNote: Iran International citing sources. Iran publicly denies. Dual-track diplomacy (public denial/private engagement) is historically consistent with Iranian negotiating patterns.

text: Iran appointed hardline IRGC veteran Mohammad-Bagher Zolghadr as the new Supreme National Security Council secretary — replacing Ali Larijani who was killed in an Israeli strike. Zolghadr was previously associated with Basij crackdown operations. His appointment signals Iran intends to continue military posture and reject any negotiated terms that don't meet its full conditions. He is almost certainly now on Israel's targeting list.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Bloomberg and The National.

text: CENTCOM Adm. Cooper confirms Hormuz is 'physically open' but Iran firing on vessels — US planning a weeks-long operation to force full reopening. Cooper stated Iran has 'attacked civilian targets more than 300 times.' Total US naval operations to date: 19 Iranian ships and 1 submarine destroyed, ~2,000 targets struck, 9,000 combat sorties flown. US military losses: 13 KIA, ~200 wounded.
verificationNote: C-SPAN CENTCOM press conference confirmed.

text: Day 25 Lebanon situation: Hezbollah fired ~30 rockets at Haifa Bay. IDF launched 7 overnight strikes on Beirut — one of the largest Beirut salvos this war. IDF also bombed gas stations across Lebanon claiming they fund Hezbollah. IDF captured 2 elite Radwan Force operatives in S. Lebanon over the weekend. Israeli FM Katz: all bridges over the Litani River destroyed. 1 million Lebanese displaced — 20% of the country.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog and France 24 confirmed all figures.

text: DEADLIEST IRAQ STRIKE: US airstrike hit the PMF operations HQ at Habbaniyah base during a command meeting, killing Anbar operations commander Saad Dawai al-Baiji and 14 fighters. At least 30 wounded. The Islamic Resistance in Iraq has now simultaneously announced a $100K bounty for US military positions while claiming 290+ total attacks since the war began.
verificationNote: PMF confirmed deaths. The National, Kurdistan 24, The Defense Post all confirmed independently.

text: OSINTdefender: IDF overnight strike on Isfahan targeted Iran’s main explosives production facility — over 120 bombs across dozens of sites including missile launch infrastructure. This factory had been partially restored after the June 2025 strike and was back in operation. IDF is now systematically re-hitting previously struck sites that Iran has repaired, suggesting the campaign is entering a “mowing the lawn” phase.
verificationNote: IDF confirmed. Times of Israel and Iran International reported.

text: Aurora Intel: Iran fired a cluster warhead ballistic missile at central Israel today, scattering bomblets over Petah Tikva and Bnei Brak — 6 wounded by shrapnel. Iran has now used cluster munitions on populated Israeli areas at least 5 confirmed times this conflict. Meanwhile Hezbollah killed a woman in her 30s at Mahanayim Junction with a rocket. Israel’s total confirmed civilian deaths from this conflict now stand at 17.
verificationNote: Cluster warhead confirmed by JPost and Al Jazeera. Mahanayim death confirmed by MDA and Ynet.

text: CTP-ISW: Houthis struck Chinese-owned tanker MV Huang Pu with 6 anti-ship missiles; 1 direct hit, fire extinguished. Notably the vessel was carrying Russian crude oil. The Houthis remain operationally active despite not conducting the “major retaliation” campaign they promised when the Iran war began. Their targeting reflects opportunism, not a coherent campaign tied to Iran’s operational calendar.
verificationNote: Houthi attack confirmed by FDD Long War Journal.

text: ISW Day 25 assessment: Trump credited Hegseth as the man who pushed hardest for the Iran strike, consistent with reporting that Hegseth pushed military options while Rubio handled diplomacy. Trump’s approval rating fell to 36% — lowest of his second term. The 5-day ceasefire window closes March 28. Islamabad talks reportedly still being arranged despite Iran’s public denials. Iran’s new SNSC chief Zolghadr is a Basij hardliner, not a negotiator.
verificationNote: ISW assessment based on confirmed public reporting from multiple outlets.

text: Iran International: Despite all public denials, Araghchi privately signaled to Witkoff and Kushner that Mojtaba Khamenei has approved deal talks if conditions are met. But Iran’s appointment of hardliner Zolghadr as SNSC chief signals the military faction is in control. Dual-track: negotiate privately, escalate publicly. Iran is trying to extract maximum concessions before publicly acknowledging any talks — a pattern consistent with 2015 JCPOA negotiations.
verificationNote: Iran International exclusive sourcing on Araghchi-Witkoff call. Zolghadr appointment confirmed by Bloomberg.

text: ELINT News: MAJOR — IDF strikes confirmed the IRGC’s main central security headquarters in Tehran. This is separate from earlier strikes on Tehran IRGC regional HQ. Combined with the 82nd Airborne deployment and Iran’s missile waves at Eilat and Jerusalem, Day 25 PM is the most kinetically active period of the afternoon watch so far.
verificationNote: IDF IRGC HQ strike confirmed by JNS and Alma Research Center.

text: OSINT Defender: Trump’s “war has been won” claim is creating massive confusion. Iran’s IRGC just called him a “deceitful president.” Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf says the peace talk claims are “fakenews to manipulate oil markets.” Yet Araghchi reportedly told Witkoff and Kushner he had Mojtaba Khamenei’s blessing to close talks. Dual-track diplomacy: deny publicly, negotiate privately.
verificationNote: Araghchi-Witkoff back-channel reported by Iran International. Ghalibaf/IRGC public denials confirmed.

text: Combat Air Force Watch: The deployment of 82nd Airborne (2,000-3,000 paratroopers) to the Middle East alongside 2 MEUs (~5,000 Marines) gives CENTCOM an airborne assault capability that didn’t previously exist in theater. This is not a defensive force. The 82nd Airborne is an offensive rapid-deployment division. Combined with the Hormuz reopening operation CENTCOM’s Admiral Cooper is planning, ground options are being actively built.
verificationNote: Deployment facts confirmed. Tactical assessment is analyst commentary.

text: Babak Taghvaee: Bushehr strike confirmed by IAEA — a structure 350 metres from the reactor was destroyed. This is the second attack near Bushehr in one week. The IAEA reports no radiation release and no reactor damage. However, the escalatory signal is unmistakable: strikes are creeping closer to Iran’s only nuclear power plant. Iran’s Atomic Energy Organization has called for emergency IAEA Board action.
verificationNote: Bushehr proximity confirmed by IAEA and Haaretz Day 25.

text: Conflicts Watcher: Day 25 PM summary — Iran fired 13+ missile/drone barrages at Israel today including cluster warheads on central Israel. IDF destroyed IRGC central HQ and hit Bushehr proximity. US killed 15 PMF fighters in Iraq’s deadliest US strike. 82nd Airborne deploying. Trump claims peace, Iran denies. Israel fears premature ceasefire. The conflict shows no signs of stopping despite diplomatic noise.
verificationNote: Summary sourced from confirmed reporting across TOI, JPost, AJ, CNN, NPR.
